steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the passage as a whole does. Look for the overall structure: the passage introduces a widespread belief, explains why it exists, then uses "But" to shift to contradictory evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: invents a novel methodology that does not appear in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B: frames the belief as something demonstrated rather than challenged — the passage does the opposite. ✗
  • Choice C: identifies the three-part structure correctly — introduce a belief, explain its prevalence, present contradictory evidence. The word "But" marks that contradictory turn. ✓
  • Choice D: treats the contradictory research as an exception rather than as the central challenge the passage builds toward. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
